calendar.matters:
Petition To Encumber Asset Held In Endowment Trust
Off Calendar Nov-24-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Erik Dryburgh for Cogswell Polytechnical College. Dr. Haskell of Cogswell Polytechnical College.

The Court informed counsel that all future filings should use the lead case number 289306 (case numbers 291628, 289306, and 291307 were consolidated). Dr. Haskell presented argument to support his position stating that the Western Association of Schools and Colleges was urging him to obtain a greater capacity; they were worried about the economy. He further stated that due to the economy it is difficult for parents to obtain loans, enrollment is down, contributions are down, etc. This petition would give them a "cushion" for Spring and Summer. Mr. Dryburgh stated that if the school loses its accredidation then the school is put on probation and students won't enroll. The Court inquired as to the current value of the property. Dr. Haskell stated that it was previously appraised at $10,100,000. The Court took the matter under submission, noting that it was leaning towards granting the petition.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Linda Forster, CSR#13286. Clerk: Jackie Alameda/ S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Petition To Encumber Asset Held In Endowment Trust
Off Calendar Nov-04-2008 Continued To Nov-24-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
